title: Mince pie traybake with frangipane
subtitle: Christmas frangipane mince-pie in slab form
excerpt: Shortcrust pastry lining a rectangular tin, spread with mincemeat, topped with an almond frangipane that bakes to a golden, nutty surface. Serves twenty as a tray cut into fingers, and avoids the fiddly individual-tin approach entirely.
sourceNotes: Mince pies are a British Christmas tradition documented from at least the sixteenth century, when they were savoury. The sweet version with fruit mincemeat is documented in British cookery from the eighteenth century onward. The traybake-with-frangipane format is a twentieth-century adaptation that simplifies batch production. No single public-domain source; the shortcrust and frangipane ratios are from standard British baking practice.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the baked tray uncut, well wrapped. Defrost overnight at room temperature and dust with icing sugar before cutting.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bakes well 2 days ahead. Store at room temperature, covered. Dust with icing sugar just before serving.

glossaryTerms.definition: A baked almond cream made from butter, sugar, ground almonds, and eggs. It rises to a light, nutty, slightly chewy layer in the oven and is the traditional topping for Bakewell tart and a common partner to mincemeat.

content.text: The mince pie traybake delivers everything the individual mince pie does: shortcrust, mincemeat,
in a format that takes a fraction of the time and feeds a crowd from a single baking session. The frangipane topping is more forgiving than pastry lids and seals the mincemeat so it stays moist.

Rub the cold butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Mix in the egg yolk and enough cold water to bring the dough together — 1–2 tablespoons; add by the spoonful. Press into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

On a lightly floured surface, roll the pastry to about 3 mm thick and large enough to line a 30 × 20 cm baking tin with a little overhang. Press into the tin, including up the sides, and trim neatly. Refrigerate for 15 minutes. Preheat the oven to 190°C conventional (170°C fan).

Spread the mincemeat evenly over the pastry base. To make the frangipane, beat the softened butter and caster sugar together until creamy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then fold in the ground almonds, flour, and almond extract. Spread the frangipane evenly over the mincemeat and scatter the flaked almonds over the top.

Bake for 30–35 minutes until the frangipane is a deep golden brown, the sides have pulled slightly from the tin, and a skewer through the frangipane layer comes out clean. Cool in the tin, then dust generously with icing sugar and cut into fingers. Serve warm or at room temperature.
